text: A Lament for the Princes of Israel

segments.text: As for you, take up a lament for the princes of Israel
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text:
and say:
‘What was your mother? A lioness among the lions! She lay down among the young lions; she reared her cubs.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: She brought up one of her cubs, and he became a young lion. After learning to tear his prey, he devoured men.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: When the nations heard of him, he was trapped in their pit. With hooks they led him awayto the land of Egypt.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: When she saw that she had waited in vain, that her hope was lost, she took another of her cubs and made him a young lion.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He prowled among the lions, and became a young lion. After learning to tear his prey, he devoured men.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He broke down their strongholds[^39] and devastated their cities. The land and everything in itshuddered at the sound of his roaring.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Then the nations set out against himfrom the provinces on every side. They spread their net over him; he was trapped in their pit.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
With hooks they caged him and brought him to the king of Babylon. They brought him into captivityso that his roar was heard no longer
on the mountains of Israel.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Your mother was like a vine in your vineyard,[^40] planted by the water; it was fruitful and full of branchesbecause of the abundant waters.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: It had strong branches, fit for a ruler’s scepter. It towered high above the thick branches, conspicuous for its height and for its dense foliage.
segments.speaker: Unknown
Unknown

segments.text: Now it is planted in the wilderness, in a dry and thirsty land.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
Fire has gone out from its main branch and devoured its fruit; on it no strong branch remainsfit for a ruler’s scepter.’
This is a lament and shall be used as a lament.
segments.speaker: Unknown
